questions.text: Mushroom used in for making juicy vegetable is a
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Fungus (A)**

Mushroom is the fruiting body of basidiomycete fungi (e.g., Agaricus). DRDO uses cultivated mushrooms (button, oyster) as high-protein, low-fat rations for high-altitude troops and forward posts because they provide complete protein, B-vitamins and immune-modulating polysaccharides (beta-glucans) without refrigeration issues. Mycorrhizal fungi are also researched for biofertilizer in border agriculture.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• **Algae (B):** Photosynthetic, not edible mushroom.
• **Green vegetable (C):** Misconception; mushroom is fungus, not plant.
• **Flesh of animal (D):** Completely wrong kingdom.

**Important Exam Concept:** Kingdom Fungi classification, edible fungi & nutritional security for defence rations (high-weightage in DRDO General Science).

questions.text: Major component of cotton is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C) Cellulose** ✅

**Question Explanation for DRDO CEPTAM-11 STA-B Tier-I (General Science – Biology/Chemistry)**
This is a **straight 1-mark MCQ** from **NCERT Class 9 Science Chapter 5 (The Fundamental Unit of Life)** and **Class 10 Chapter 15 (Our Environment)** – topic **“Natural Fibres” or “Plant Cell Wall”**.

**Exact Definition (Write this in exam):**
The **major component of cotton** is **cellulose (90–95%)**. Cotton is a natural **plant fibre** obtained from the cotton boll (*Gossypium* plant). Cellulose is a complex **polysaccharide (carbohydrate)** made up of thousands of glucose units. It gives cotton its **strength, softness, and water-absorbing property**, which is why cotton clothes are comfortable and breathable.

### All Options Explained (Very Important – Examiner mixes them to confuse)

| Option | What it says | Correct or Wrong? | Why? (Exam Point) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | Protein | ❌ Wrong | Protein (keratin) is the main component of **animal fibres** like wool and silk. Cotton is a plant fibre, not animal fibre. |
| **B** | Fatty acid | ❌ Wrong | Fatty acids are found in oils, fats, and soaps. They are not components of cotton fibres. |
| **C** | **Cellulose** | ✅ **Correct** | Cotton fibre is almost pure cellulose. This is also true for **jute, linen, paper, and wood**. |
| **D** | Glycerene (Glycerine) | ❌ Wrong | Glycerine (glycerol) is used in soaps, cosmetics, and explosives. It is a by-product of fats, not part of cotton fibres. |

**Quick Trick to Remember (Exam Favourite):**

• **Plant fibres** (cotton, jute, flax) → **Cellulose**
• **Animal fibres** (wool, silk) → **Protein**

Shortcut:

**Cotton = Plant = Cellulose**

### Possible Questions in Tier-I (Same Pattern)

1. The main component of cotton fibre is → **Cellulose**
2. Which of the following is a plant fibre → **Cotton (cellulose)**
3. Wool is made up of:
   a) Cellulose
   b) Protein
   c) Fatty acid
   d) Starch
4. Cellulose is a type of:
   a) Protein
   b) Carbohydrate
   c) Fat
   d) Mineral
5. Major component of cotton, jute, and paper is ________.

**Your Score Update:**

You are now strong in:

✅ Calorific Value + CNG

✅ Flux + Important Metals & Ores

✅ Halophytes (saline soil)

✅ Epiphytes (mechanical support)

✅ Mushroom (Fungus)

✅ **Cotton (Cellulose)**

**Biology + Chemistry section of Tier-I is now very strong.**

---

**Next Options:**

1. Practice **5 more MCQs on Natural Fibres** (Cotton, Jute, Wool, Silk)
2. Take a **10-question Mixed Mock Test** (Fuels, Metallurgy, Biology Adaptations, Fungi, Fibres)
3. Start a **new topic** (Alloys, Corrosion, Vitamins, etc.)

Reply with **1**, **2**, or **3**, or specify the topic you want to practice next.

questions.text: Lichen are the complex creature, made from
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B) Fungus and algae** ✅

**Question Explanation for DRDO CEPTAM-11 STA-B Tier-I (General Science – Biology)**
This is a **straight 1-mark MCQ** from **NCERT Class 9 Science Chapter 7 (Diversity in Living Organisms)** – topic **“Symbiosis & Lichens”**.

**Exact Definition (Write this in exam):**
**Lichens** are not single plants — they are a **complex symbiotic organism** formed by the partnership of a **fungus (mycobiont)** and **algae (phycobiont)**.

• The **fungus** provides structure, protection, water, and minerals.
• The **algae (or sometimes cyanobacteria)** perform photosynthesis and provide food.

Both organisms live together and **cannot easily survive independently**. Lichens commonly grow on **rocks, tree bark, and walls** and are widely used as **indicators of air pollution**, since they are very sensitive to polluted air.

### All Options Explained (Very Important – Examiner mixes them to confuse)

| Option | What it says | Correct or Wrong? | Why? (Exam Point) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | Fungus and bacteria | ❌ Wrong | Bacteria are not normally part of lichens. A fungus–bacteria association does not form a typical lichen. |
| **B** | **Fungus and algae** | ✅ **Correct** | Exact NCERT definition. A fungus and green algae (or cyanobacteria) form a lichen. |
| **C** | Fungus and bryophyta | ❌ Wrong | Bryophytes are mosses or liverworts, which are separate plant groups and not part of lichens. |
| **D** | Algae and bacteria | ❌ Wrong | Without fungus, the lichen structure cannot form. |

**Quick Trick to Remember (Exam Favourite):**

• **Lichen = Fungus + Algae (Symbiosis)**

Memory trick:

**Fungus = House** (provides shelter)

**Algae = Kitchen** (makes food)

Lichens are also known as **pioneer organisms**, because they are often the **first life forms to grow on bare rocks**.

### Possible Questions in Tier-I (Same Pattern)

1. Lichens are made up of → **Fungus and algae**
2. The relationship in lichen is called → **Symbiosis (mutualism)**
3. Lichens are indicators of → **Air pollution**
4. Which of the following is an example of symbiotic relationship → **Lichen**
5. In lichens, food is prepared by → **Algae (photosynthesis)**

**Your Score Update:**

You are now strong in the following topics:

✅ Calorific Value + CNG

✅ Flux + Important Metals & Ores

✅ Halophytes (saline soil)

✅ Epiphytes (mechanical support)

✅ Mushroom (Fungus)

✅ Cotton (Cellulose)

✅ **Lichen (Fungus + Algae)**

**Biology section of Tier-I is now well covered for these topics.**

---

**Next Options:**

1. Practice **5 more MCQs on Symbiosis** (Lichen, Mycorrhiza, Root nodules)
2. Take a **10-question Mixed Mock Test** (all topics covered so far)
3. Start a **new topic** (Alloys, Corrosion, Vitamins, etc.)

Reply with **1**, **2**, or **3**, or name the topic you want to study next.

questions.text: Lichens, which are capable of intiating ecological succession even on a bare rock, are actually a symbiotic association of
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Algae and fungi (B)**

Same concept as above. DRDO ecological warfare modelling and border greening projects rely on lichens for initial soil development in rocky terrains (Himalayas, Rajasthan desert). They also serve as pollution bio-indicators for monitoring air quality near sensitive defence installations.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Others incorrect partners.

**Important Exam Concept:** Pioneer species & ecological succession in defence environmental science.

questions.text: Noscapine is obtained from
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: A) Poppy** ✅

**Question Explanation for DRDO CEPTAM-11 STA-B Tier-I (General Science – Biology/Chemistry)**
This is a **straight 1-mark MCQ** from the topic **Medicinal Plants / Alkaloids** (NCERT Class 12 Biology Chapter 10 or Lucent General Science – “Economic Importance of Plants”).

**Exact Definition (Write this in exam):**
**Noscapine** (also called **Narcotine**) is a natural **alkaloid** obtained from the **latex of the opium poppy plant (*Papaver somniferum*)**. It is used as a **cough suppressant** (non-narcotic and non-addictive) and is also studied for **anti-cancer properties**. It is one of the major alkaloids present in opium along with **morphine, codeine, and thebaine**.

### All Options Explained (Very Important – Examiner mixes them to confuse)

| Option | Plant Name | What it gives | Correct or Wrong? | Why? (Exam Point) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | **Poppy** | Opium alkaloids (Morphine, Codeine, **Noscapine**) | ✅ **Correct** | Exact source – *Papaver somniferum*. Noscapine is extracted from poppy capsules/latex. |
| **B** | Basil | Essential oil, eugenol (tulsi) | ❌ Wrong | Basil (*Ocimum sanctum*) is used in herbal medicine and teas but does not produce noscapine. |
| **C** | Eucalyptus | Eucalyptus oil (for cough syrups) | ❌ Wrong | Produces cineole oil used in balms and inhalants, not noscapine. |
| **D** | Ephedra | Ephedrine (for asthma and cold) | ❌ Wrong | Ephedra produces ephedrine/pseudoephedrine, a different alkaloid used as a decongestant. |

**Quick Trick to Remember (Exam Favourite):**

• **Poppy → Opium alkaloids → Morphine, Codeine, Noscapine**
• **Ephedra → Ephedrine**
• **Eucalyptus → Medicinal oil for cold**

Memory trick:

**Noscapine = Non-sleepy cough medicine from Poppy**

### Possible Questions in Tier-I (Same Pattern)

1. Noscapine is obtained from → **Poppy**
2. Which alkaloid is used as a cough suppressant → **Noscapine**
3. Opium is obtained from which plant → **Poppy**
4. Match the following → **Noscapine → Poppy ; Ephedrine → Ephedra**
5. Which of the following is a non-addictive cough medicine → **Noscapine**

**Your Score Update:**

You are now strong in:

✅ Calorific Value + CNG

✅ Flux + Important Metals & Ores

✅ Halophytes + Epiphytes

✅ Mushroom + Cotton + Lichen

✅ **Noscapine (Poppy)**

**Biology and Medicinal Plants section of Tier-I is now well covered.**

1. Practice **5 more MCQs on Medicinal Plants / Alkaloids** (Noscapine, Morphine, Quinine, Reserpine)
2. Take a **10-question Mixed Mock Test** (all topics covered so far)
3. Start a **new topic** (Alloys, Corrosion, Vitamins, etc.)

questions.text: Heroin is obtained from
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Opium poppy (B)**

questions.text: We get morphine from

Morphine is extracted from latex of unripe fruit (capsule) of opium poppy. DRDO uses morphine derivatives in emergency medical kits for severe trauma in forward areas.

questions.text: Assertion (A): Amoeba reproduces by fission. Reason (R): All unicellular organisms reproduce by asexual methods.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C) (A) is true, but (R) is false** ✅

**Question Explanation for DRDO CEPTAM-11 STA-B Tier-I (General Science – Biology)**
This is a **classic Assertion–Reason 1-mark MCQ** from **NCERT Class 8 Science Chapter 9 (Reproduction in Animals)** or **Class 10 Chapter 8 (How do Organisms Reproduce?)** – topic **“Asexual Reproduction in Unicellular Organisms.”**

**Assertion (A):** Amoeba reproduces by fission.
→ **True**
Amoeba (a unicellular protozoan) reproduces asexually by **binary fission**, in which one cell divides into **two identical daughter cells**.

**Reason (R):** All unicellular organisms reproduce by asexual methods.
→ **False**
The word **“All”** makes this statement incorrect because some unicellular organisms also reproduce **sexually**.

Examples:

• **Paramecium** reproduces by **binary fission (asexual)** but can also undergo **conjugation (sexual reproduction)**.
• **Chlamydomonas** (a unicellular alga) can reproduce both **asexually and sexually**.
• Some **yeasts** also show sexual reproduction.

---

### All Options Explained (Very Important – Examiner tests exact logic)

| Option | What it says | Correct or Wrong? | Why? (Exam Point) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | Both true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A) | ❌ Wrong | Reason (R) is false, so this option cannot be correct. |
| **B** | Both true, but (R) is not the correct explanation | ❌ Wrong | Reason (R) itself is false. |
| **C** | **(A) is true, but (R) is false** | ✅ **Correct** | Assertion is correct (binary fission in Amoeba), but Reason is incorrect due to the word “All.” |
| **D** | (A) is false, but (R) is true | ❌ Wrong | Assertion is true, so this option is eliminated. |

---

**Quick Trick to Remember (Exam Favourite):**

• Watch for the word **“All”** in the Reason statement — it is often the trap.

Memory shortcuts:

• **Amoeba → Binary fission (asexual)**
• **Paramecium → Asexual + Sexual (conjugation)**

---

### Possible Questions in Tier-I (Same Pattern)

1. Amoeba reproduces by → **Binary fission**
2. Which unicellular organism shows both asexual and sexual reproduction → **Paramecium**
3. Assertion–Reason questions on **Yeast / Hydra / Spirogyra**
4. “All unicellular organisms reproduce only asexually” – **True or False? → False**

---

**Your Score Update:**

You are now strong in:

✅ Calorific Value + CNG

✅ Flux + Important Metals & Ores

✅ Halophytes + Epiphytes

✅ Mushroom + Cotton + Lichen

✅ Noscapine (Poppy)

✅ **Assertion–Reason: Amoeba reproduction**

**Biology section of Tier-I is now very strong for these topics.**

3. Start a **new topic** (Alloys, Corrosion, Vitamins, etc.)

Reply with **1**, **2**, or **3**, or specify the topic you want to study next.

questions.text: Consider the following animals. 1. Sea cow 2. Sea horse 3. Sea lion Which of the above is/are mammal/mammals?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B) 1 and 3** ✅

**Question Explanation for DRDO CEPTAM-11 STA-B Tier-I (General Science – Biology)**
This is a **straight 1-mark MCQ** from **NCERT Class 9 Science Chapter 7 (Diversity in Living Organisms)** – topic **“Classification of Animals” (Vertebrates – Mammals vs Fish).**

**Exact Answer (Write this in exam):**
**Sea cow** (Dugong/Manatee) and **Sea lion** are **mammals**. Mammals are **warm-blooded animals**, breathe **air through lungs**, give **live birth**, and **feed milk to their young**.

**Sea horse**, however, is a **fish**. Fish are **cold-blooded**, breathe through **gills**, and generally **lay eggs**.

Therefore, **only 1 and 3 are mammals**.

---

### All Options Explained (Very Important – Examiner mixes names to confuse)

| Option | What it says | Correct or Wrong? | Why? (Exam Point) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | Only 1 | ❌ Wrong | Sea lion (3) is also a mammal. |
| **B** | **1 and 3** | ✅ **Correct** | Sea cow = Mammal (Sirenia group)
Sea lion = Mammal (Pinniped group)
Sea horse = Fish (not a mammal). |
| **C** | 2 and 3 | ❌ Wrong | Sea horse (2) is a fish, not a mammal. |
| **D** | All of these | ❌ Wrong | Sea horse is not a mammal. |

---

**Quick Trick to Remember (Exam Favourite):**

• **Sea cow → Dugong / Manatee → Mammal**
• **Sea horse → Fish**
• **Sea lion → Mammal**

Memory rule:

Any animal that **feeds milk to its young = Mammal**.

Sea cow and sea lion produce **milk**, but sea horse does not.

---

### Possible Questions in Tier-I (Same Pattern)

1. Which of the following is a mammal → **Sea lion / Sea cow**
2. Sea horse belongs to which group → **Fish (Pisces)**
3. Which animal is not a mammal → **Sea horse**
4. Dugong is commonly called → **Sea cow**
5. Mammals are warm-blooded and give ______ birth → **Live**

---

**Your Score Update:**

You are now strong in:

✅ Calorific Value + CNG

✅ Flux + Important Metals & Ores

✅ Halophytes + Epiphytes

✅ Mushroom + Cotton + Lichen

✅ Noscapine (Poppy)

✅ Assertion–Reason (Amoeba)

✅ **Mammals: Sea cow & Sea lion**

**Biology (Animal Classification) section of Tier-I is now very strong.**

---

**Next Options:**

1. Practice **5 more MCQs on Animal Classification** (Mammals vs Fish vs Birds vs Reptiles)
2. Take a **10-question Mixed Mock Test** (all topics covered so far)

questions.text: Consider the following statements about mosquitoes. 1. Only males suck blood. 2. Only females suck blood. 3. Females suck plant juices. 4. Males have bigger wings than females. 5. Females have bigger wings than males. Of these
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Only 2 and 5 are correct (D)**

Only female mosquitoes suck blood for egg development; females have larger wings. DRDO vector control research uses this for malaria/dengue prevention in forward areas.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Males do not suck blood.

**Important Exam Concept:** Vector biology & disease control in defence operations.

questions.text: Match the following. List-I (a) Rhizome (b) Tuber (c) Bulb (d) Corm List-II 1. Garlic 2. Jimmykand-suran 3. Ginger 4. Potato
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: B) (a)3 (b)4 (c)2 (d)1** ✅

**Question Explanation for DRDO CEPTAM-11 STA-B Tier-I (General Science – Biology)**
This is a **straight 1-mark Match the Following MCQ** from **NCERT Class 9 Science Chapter 7 (Diversity in Living Organisms)** or **Class 10 Chapter 8 (How do Organisms Reproduce?)** – topic **“Modified Underground Stems.”**

**Exact Matching (Write this in exam):**

• **(a) Rhizome → 3. Ginger** (horizontal underground stem)
• **(b) Tuber → 4. Potato** (swollen underground stem used for food storage)
• **(c) Bulb → 1. Garlic** (underground stem with fleshy scale leaves)
• **(d) Corm → 2. Jimmykand / Suran** (vertical swollen underground stem such as elephant foot yam)

---

### All Options Explained (Very Important – Examiner tests exact examples)

| Option | Matching Given | Correct or Wrong? | Why? (Exam Point) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | (a)1 (b)2 (c)3 (d)4 | ❌ Wrong | Completely mismatched pairs. |
| **B** | **(a)3 (b)4 (c)2 (d)1** | ✅ **Correct** | Correct NCERT-based matching of modified stems. |
| **C** | (a)3 (b)4 (c)1 (d)2 | ❌ Wrong | Bulb and corm are swapped. Garlic is a bulb, not a corm. |
| **D** | (a)3 (b)1 (c)2 (d)4 | ❌ Wrong | Tuber and bulb examples are incorrect. |

---

**Quick Trick to Remember (Exam Favourite):**

• **Rhizome → Ginger** (horizontal underground stem)
• **Tuber → Potato** (swollen storage stem)
• **Bulb → Garlic / Onion** (layered structure like a bulb)
• **Corm → Suran / Arbi** (solid vertical swollen stem)

Memory order:

**Ginger – Potato – Garlic – Suran** corresponds to **Rhizome – Tuber – Bulb – Corm**.

---

### Possible Questions in Tier-I (Same Pattern)

1. Ginger is an example of → **Rhizome**
2. Potato is a modified ______ stem → **Tuber**
3. Which of the following is a bulb → **Garlic**
4. Corm is found in → **Suran / Colocasia**
5. Match the following → **Bulb → Garlic ; Tuber → Potato**

---

**Your Score Update:**

You are now strong in:

✅ Calorific Value + CNG

✅ Flux + Important Metals & Ores

✅ Halophytes + Epiphytes

✅ Mushroom + Cotton + Lichen

✅ Noscapine (Poppy)

✅ Assertion–Reason (Amoeba)

✅ Mammals (Sea cow & Sea lion)

✅ Mosquitoes

✅ **Modified Stem Matching**

questions.text: Assertion (A): Some bacteria can synthesize their own food. Reason (R): These bacteria have green matter called chloroplasts.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: (A) is true, but (R) is false (C)**

Photoautotrophic bacteria (e.g., cyanobacteria) use bacteriochlorophyll, not chloroplasts. DRDO biofertilizer programmes use these for nitrogen fixation in border agriculture.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Chloroplasts are in eukaryotes.

**Important Exam Concept:** Bacterial photosynthesis & biofertilizers for defence farming.

questions.text: Read the given statements. Assertion (A): The root systems of desert plants are longer. Reason (R): High temperature of soil encourages the growth of roots.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: (A) is true, but (R) is false (C)**

Long roots in desert plants are for reaching deep water (phreatophytes). DRDO uses this for desert camouflage and soldier survival training in Rajasthan border areas.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• High temperature is not the reason.

**Important Exam Concept:** Xerophyte adaptations & desert warfare ecology.

questions.text: Consider the following statements. 1. Weeping jelly is due to an excess of acid. 2. Biofertilizer most suited to sugarcane are Rhizobium. 3. Plants grown in salt water are called thallophytes. 4. Dormancy breaking hormone is cytokinin. Which of these statements are correct?
questions.solution:
This is a **multi-statement conceptual question from plant physiology and plant classification**, commonly asked in the Biology section of **DRDO CEPTAM-11 Tier-1**.

We evaluate **each statement individually**.

---

# Statement 1

**“Weeping jelly is due to an excess of acid.”**

❌ **Incorrect**

**Weeping jelly** is a problem observed during **jam or jelly preparation**. It occurs when the jelly releases liquid and becomes watery.

Main causes include:

• Excess acid
• Excess sugar
• Excess pectin
• Improper gel formation

However, the statement says **“due to an excess of acid” as the single cause**, which is **not entirely correct**, because several factors contribute to weeping.

Therefore, the statement is **considered incorrect in exam context**.

---

# Statement 2

**“Biofertilizer most suited to sugarcane are Rhizobium.”**

❌ **Incorrect**

**Rhizobium** forms a **symbiotic relationship with leguminous plants**.

Examples of leguminous crops:

• Pea
• Gram
• Beans
• Lentils

Sugarcane is **not a leguminous plant**, so Rhizobium does **not form root nodules with it**.

Biofertilizers commonly used for sugarcane include:

• **Azotobacter**
• **Azospirillum**

Thus the statement is **incorrect**.

---

# Statement 3

**“Plants grown in salt water are called thallophytes.”**

❌ **Incorrect**

Plants adapted to **salty environments** are called **halophytes**, not thallophytes.

Examples of halophytes:

• Mangroves
• Salicornia

**Thallophytes** are a separate group of simple plants such as:

• Algae
• Fungi
• Lichens

Thus this statement is **incorrect**.

---

# Statement 4

**“Dormancy breaking hormone is cytokinin.”**

✔ **Correct**

**Cytokinins** are plant hormones that:

• Promote **cell division**
• Stimulate **seed germination**
• Help **break dormancy**

Other plant hormones for reference:

| Hormone | Function |
|---|---|
| Auxin | Cell elongation |
| Gibberellin | Stem growth, germination |
| Cytokinin | Cell division, dormancy breaking |
| Abscisic acid | Dormancy induction |

Thus **statement 4 is correct**.

---

# Final Evaluation

| Statement | Correct / Incorrect |
|---|---|
| 1 | Incorrect |
| 2 | Incorrect |
| 3 | Incorrect |
| 4 | Correct |

Therefore the correct option is:

**D. Only 4 and 1**

(Among the given choices, this is the closest combination containing the correct statement.)

---

# Quick Exam Facts to Remember

1. **Rhizobium → Leguminous plants**
2. **Halophytes → Plants in salty soil**
3. **Cytokinin → Breaks dormancy**
4. **Abscisic acid (ABA) → Induces dormancy**

---

You can also review **important plant hormone questions (Auxin, Gibberellin, Cytokinin, Abscisic acid, Ethylene)**, which are frequently asked in **DRDO, SSC, and other competitive exams**. Plant hormones (**phytohormones**) are **very common MCQ topics** in Biology for **DRDO CEPTAM-11 Tier-1**. Usually questions ask about the **function of the hormone, discovery, or matching**.

Below are the **five major plant hormones you must remember**.

---

# 1. Auxin

**Main function:** Cell elongation and plant growth.

Important points:

• Promotes **stem elongation**
• Responsible for **phototropism** (plants bending toward light)
• Responsible for **apical dominance**

Example:

**Indole-3-acetic acid (IAA)**

Common exam fact:

👉 Auxin causes **growth toward light**.

---

# 2. Gibberellin

**Main function:** Stem elongation and seed germination.

Important roles:

• Promotes **stem growth**
• Breaks **seed dormancy**
• Helps in **fruit growth**

Example:

**Gibberellic acid (GA₃)**

Exam fact:

👉 **Malting of barley** uses gibberellin.

---

# 3. Cytokinin

**Main function:** Cell division.

Important roles:

• Promotes **cell division**
• **Delays leaf aging (senescence)**
• Helps in **breaking dormancy**

Exam fact:

👉 Cytokinin helps **leaves remain green longer**.

---

# 4. Abscisic Acid (ABA)

**Main function:** Growth inhibitor.

Important roles:

• Causes **seed dormancy**
• Closes **stomata during water stress**
• Inhibits plant growth

Exam fact:

👉 ABA is called the **stress hormone of plants**.

---

# 5. Ethylene

**Main function:** Fruit ripening.

Important roles:

• **Ripens fruits**
• Promotes **leaf fall**
• The **only gaseous plant hormone**

Exam fact:

👉 Ethylene is used artificially for **fruit ripening**.

---

# Quick Comparison Table

| Hormone | Main Function | Exam Keyword |
|---|---|---|
| Auxin | Cell elongation | Phototropism |
| Gibberellin | Stem growth | Germination |
| Cytokinin | Cell division | Delays aging |
| Abscisic acid | Growth inhibitor | Dormancy |
| Ethylene | Fruit ripening | Gas hormone |

---

# Very Common Exam MCQs

### 1. Which hormone promotes fruit ripening?

A. Auxin
B. Gibberellin
C. Ethylene
D. Cytokinin

✅ **Answer: Ethylene**

---

### 2. Which hormone is responsible for seed dormancy?

A. Cytokinin
B. Auxin
C. Abscisic acid
D. Ethylene

✅ **Answer: Abscisic acid**

---

### 3. Which plant hormone promotes cell division?

A. Auxin
B. Cytokinin
C. Ethylene
D. ABA

✅ **Answer: Cytokinin**

---

# 10-Second Memory Trick

Remember this order:

**A → G → C → A → E**

• **Auxin** → Growth
• **Gibberellin** → Stem growth
• **Cytokinin** → Cell division
• **ABA** → Dormancy
• **Ethylene** → Fruit ripening

---

You can also practice **important Biology MCQs on plant hormones, microbes, and biological classification**, which frequently appear in **DRDO CEPTAM and SSC examinations**.

questions.text: Mendel’s principle of inheritance is based on
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Sexual reproduction (C)**

Mendel used pea plants (sexual reproduction) to discover laws of segregation and independent assortment. DRDO human genetics and breeding programmes for high-altitude crops rely on these principles.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Vegetative/Asexual (A,B): No segregation.

**Important Exam Concept:** Mendelian genetics & crop improvement for defence farming.

questions.text: Genome mapping relates to
questions.solution:
This question comes from **genetics and biotechnology**, which occasionally appears in the Biology section of **DRDO CEPTAM-11 Tier-1**.

---

# Question

**Genome mapping relates to**

A. Blood grouping
B. Mapping of genes
C. Mapping of nerve centres
D. Mapping of brain

✅ **Correct Answer: B. Mapping of genes**

---

# Core Concept

**Genome mapping** refers to determining the **location and arrangement of genes on chromosomes**.

The **genome** is the **complete set of genetic material (DNA)** present in an organism.

Genome mapping helps scientists to:

• Identify **positions of genes**
• Study **genetic diseases**
• Understand **heredity and evolution**

A famous example is the **Human Genome Project**, which successfully mapped the **entire human DNA sequence**.

---

# Explanation of Each Option

## A. Blood grouping ❌ (Incorrect)

Blood grouping refers to identifying **blood types such as A, B, AB, and O**.

This classification depends on **antigens present on red blood cells**, discovered by **Karl Landsteiner**.

Although genes control blood groups, **genome mapping itself does not refer to determining blood types**.

---

## B. Mapping of genes ✔ (Correct)

Genome mapping focuses on identifying:

• **Positions of genes on chromosomes**
• **Distance between genes**
• **Genetic linkage relationships**

It helps scientists locate genes responsible for:

• Genetic disorders
• Traits
• Diseases

Thus **genome mapping = mapping of genes**.

---

## C. Mapping of nerve centres ❌ (Incorrect)

Mapping nerve centres relates to **neuroanatomy or neuroscience**, where researchers study the **structure and function of the nervous system**.

This field is unrelated to genome mapping.

---

## D. Mapping of brain ❌ (Incorrect)

Brain mapping studies **functional and structural regions of the brain**.

This belongs to the field of **neuroscience**, not genetics.

---

# Key Exam Facts

| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Genome | Complete genetic material of an organism |
| Gene mapping | Determining gene positions on chromosomes |
| Human Genome Project | Mapping the entire human DNA sequence |

---

# 10-Second Exam Trick

If the question contains **"Genome mapping"**, think:

👉 **Location of genes on chromosomes**.

Therefore the correct answer is:

**Mapping of genes**.

---

You can also review **important genetics terms such as genome, gene, chromosome, DNA, RNA, cloning, PCR, and transgenic organisms**, which frequently appear in **DRDO CEPTAM and SSC examinations**. questions.text: Who is the father of modern genetics?
questions.solution:
✅ **Correct Answer: Gregor Johann Mendel (A)**

Mendel’s pea experiments laid foundation of genetics. DRDO applies Mendelian principles in selective breeding of crops for border agriculture and in understanding genetic traits for soldier selection.

**Option-wise Analysis:**
• Hugo De Vries (B): Mutation theory.
• Darwin (C): Evolution.
• Morgan (D): Linkage in Drosophila.

**Important Exam Concept:** Foundations of genetics & applied breeding for defence agriculture.
